bugs.description: The loop uses an inclusive range 0..=len instead of exclusive 0..len. This reads one element past the end of the slice, causing undefined behavior by accessing uninitialized memory.

Creates a mutable reference from an immutable reference by casting through a raw pointer. This violates Rust's aliasing rules and causes immediate undefined behavior. The immutable reference is still live and the mutable reference would allow modifications through it.
The FastBuffer struct allocates memory with alloc() but provides no Drop implementation. This means the allocated memory is never deallocated, causing a memory leak whenever a FastBuffer is dropped.
